inputs.userGoal: Launch a cloud-based webhook-driven PDF converter using Azure Functions, persist job metadata in Cosmos DB, and use Azure Key Vault for secrets.

architecturePrinciples: Serverless-first for cost efficiency and auto-scaling
Secure secret management via Key Vault with RBAC
Managed identity for passwordless service-to-service auth

reasoning.whyChosen: Required backend for Application Insights. PerGB2018 is the standard pay-as-you-go tier suitable for serverless workloads.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Pay-per-GB ingestion cost; 30-day retention keeps cost low.

resources.name: appi-pdfconverter-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Provides distributed tracing, live metrics, and failure diagnostics for the Function App. Essential for observability in serverless architectures.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Native Azure integration vs vendor lock-in.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Application Insights requires a Log Analytics workspace as its data backend.

reasoning.whyChosen: User-assigned managed identity enables passwordless authentication from the Function App to Key Vault and Cosmos DB. Preferred over system-assigned for portability across deployment slots.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Slightly more setup than system-assigned but reusable across resources and slots.

resources.name: kv-pdfconverter-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Centralized secret management for API keys, connection strings, and third-party credentials. RBAC authorization is preferred over legacy access policies. Standard SKU sufficient (no HSM keys needed).
alternativesConsidered: App Configuration with Key Vault references
reasoning.tradeoffs: Small per-operation cost; adds latency for secret retrieval (mitigated by caching in Function App).

resources.dependencyReasoning: The managed identity needs to exist before assigning Key Vault Secrets User role.

reasoning.whyChosen: Required by Azure Functions runtime for trigger management, logging, and internal state. Also serves as blob storage for uploaded/converted PDF files. StorageV2 is mandatory for Functions (needs Queue + Table). Standard_LRS is sufficient for a single-region workload.
alternativesConsidered: Separate storage accounts for Functions vs PDFs
reasoning.tradeoffs: LRS has no zone redundancy but is cheapest. A single account simplifies management; separate accounts would improve isolation.

reasoning.whyChosen: Serverless Cosmos DB (NoSQL API) is ideal for bursty/webhook-driven workloads with unpredictable traffic. Pay only for consumed RUs. Session consistency provides read-your-writes for job status tracking. NoSQL API offers flexible schema for job metadata.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Serverless is single-region only and has a 1 GB container limit for shared throughput. Sufficient for job metadata but not for large-scale analytics.

reasoning.whyChosen: Consumption plan (Y1) provides automatic scaling and pay-per-execution pricing, ideal for webhook-driven workloads with variable traffic. Linux for broader runtime support.
alternativesConsidered: Premium EP1 for VNet integration and no cold starts
reasoning.tradeoffs: Cold starts possible under Consumption. 5-minute default execution timeout may limit large PDF conversions. Upgrade to EP1 if latency is critical.

resources.name: func-pdfconverter-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Azure Functions with HTTP trigger serves as the webhook endpoint. Serverless execution model aligns with event-driven PDF conversion. Key Vault references in app settings enable secure secret access without embedding credentials. Node.js 20 chosen as a broadly supported runtime.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Functions Consumption has cold starts and a 5-min default timeout. For large PDFs, consider upgrading to Premium plan or using Durable Functions for long-running orchestrations.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Function App requires: App Service Plan (hosting), Storage Account (runtime), Key Vault (secrets via references), Cosmos DB (job metadata), Application Insights (monitoring), and Managed Identity (authentication).

overallReasoning.summary: Fully serverless architecture using Azure Functions (Consumption) for webhook processing, Cosmos DB Serverless (NoSQL) for job metadata, Key Vault for secrets, and Application Insights for observability. A user-assigned managed identity provides passwordless auth between services. This design minimizes cost at low traffic and auto-scales under load.
overallReasoning.tradeoffs: Consumption plan cold starts may add latency to the first webhook call. The 5-minute execution timeout limits processing of very large PDFs — Durable Functions or a queue-triggered pattern can mitigate this. Cosmos DB Serverless is single-region only, so no geo-redundancy for metadata. No VNet isolation in Consumption tier — upgrade to Premium if network security is required.

plan.validation: All pairing constraints verified: Storage Account uses StorageV2 kind (compatible with Functions), Consumption plan does not reference VNet-secured storage, Cosmos DB Serverless is single-region with Session consistency (valid), Key Vault uses RBAC authorization with soft delete and purge protection enabled, and Function App references Key Vault secrets via managed identity.
